      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

packages\cli\README.md:93:openmnemo daemon status           # show registration and lock state

C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\assets\templates\en\agents.md:32:- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\assets\templates\en\agents.md:35:- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\assets\templates\en\agents.md:38:- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\package.json:11:    "memorytree-daemon": "./dist/cli.js"

C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\SKILL.md:18:5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user for their preferred heartbeat interval, auto_push preference, and scope, then offer to run `memorytree daemon install`. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\SKILL.md:35:12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\SKILL.md:37:14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\SKILL.md:38:15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\SKILL.md:96:9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\SKILL.md:97:10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only — no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:222:The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It can discover transcripts, import them, generate reports, commit repo-local memory updates, and optionally push.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:232:- On `memorytree/*` branches, the heartbeat can mirror transcripts into the repository and commit MemoryTree changes.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:233:- On non-`memorytree/*` branches, the heartbeat still imports into the global archive, but keeps the repository clean.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:235:Install the heartbeat:
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:238:node dist/cli.js daemon install
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:252:heartbeat_interval = "5m"
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:255:generate_report = true
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:271:| `generate_report` | `false` | Generate `Memory/07_reports/` during heartbeat runs |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:288:| Failure handling | Push failures alert and retry once; report deploy failures never abort the heartbeat |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:303:| `memorytree report build` | Build the static HTML report site |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:304:| `memorytree report serve` | Serve the generated report locally over HTTP |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:305:| `memorytree daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:306:| `memorytree daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:307:| `memorytree daemon run-once` | Execute one heartbeat cycle immediately |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:308:| `memorytree daemon watch` | Development-only continuous heartbeat loop |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\README.md:309:| `memorytree daemon status` | Show scheduler registration and lock status |

C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:7:Provide a single user-level location for global state, configuration, and cross-project data so that heartbeat, daemon, and multi-project features have a shared, predictable root.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:15:  heartbeat.lock           # Single-instance lock for the heartbeat process
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:17:    heartbeat-<date>.log   # Heartbeat execution logs, rotated by date
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:35:| `heartbeat_interval` | string     | `"5m"`           | Interval between heartbeat executions. Passed to the OS scheduler. |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:38:| `auto_push`          | boolean    | `true`           | Whether the heartbeat pushes after committing. See `references/git-policy.md`. |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:45:If `config.toml` contains invalid values, the heartbeat uses built-in defaults for those fields and logs a warning:
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:47:- `heartbeat_interval`: must be a positive duration string (e.g., `"5m"`, `"300s"`). Invalid or zero values fall back to `"5m"`.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:49:- `projects`: entries with non-existent `path` values are skipped during the heartbeat run (not removed from config).
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:53:If the file itself is malformed TOML, the heartbeat logs an error, uses all built-in defaults, and continues.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:85:4. **Failure threshold**: The heartbeat writes a `push_failed` alert only after 3 consecutive failures for the same project. The count resets on a successful push.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\global-configuration.md:95:- **Deregistration**: Removing a project entry stops the heartbeat from scanning it. Existing transcripts in the global archive are not deleted.

C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:11:1. `heartbeat.py` executes once and exits. It is stateless and idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:12:2. The OS-native scheduler invokes `heartbeat.py` on a fixed interval. Use cron (Linux), launchd (macOS), or Task Scheduler (Windows).
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:13:3. One computer runs exactly one scheduled heartbeat task. Multiple registrations on the same machine are not supported.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:16:6. The `memorytree-daemon watch` mode (continuous loop) exists only for development and debugging. Production use must rely on the OS scheduler.
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:20:The `memorytree-daemon` command manages the heartbeat lifecycle:
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:24:| `install`   | Register the heartbeat task with the OS scheduler at the configured interval. Refuses to overwrite an existing registration. |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:25:| `uninstall` | Remove the heartbeat task from the OS scheduler. |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:26:| `run-once`  | Execute a single heartbeat cycle immediately, then exit. |
C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow\references\heartbeat-scheduling.md:28:| `status`    | Show whether a heartbeat task is registered and its last execution result. |

Output:
/**
 * MemoryTree heartbeat 鈥?single execution, stateless, idempotent.
 * Port of scripts/heartbeat.py
 */

import type { ParsedTranscript } from '@openmnemo/types'
import { discoverSourceFiles, transcriptMatchesRepo } from '@openmnemo/core'
import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '@openmnemo/core'
import { parseTranscript } from '@openmnemo/core'
import { slugify } from '@openmnemo/core'
import { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot } from '@openmnemo/core'
import type { Config } from './config.js'
import { loadConfig } from './config.js'
import { acquireLock, releaseLock } from './lock.js'
import { resetFailureCount, writeAlert, writeAlertWithThreshold } from './alert.js'
import type { LogLevel } from './log.js'
import { getLogger, setupLogging } from './log.js'
import { git } from '@openmnemo/core'
import { toPosixPath } from '@openmnemo/core'
import { join, resolve } from 'node:path'
import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Report generation
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async function runBuildReport(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string): Promise<void> {
  const logger = getLogger()
  const output = join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports')
  try {
    const { buildReport } = await import('@openmnemo/report')
    await buildReport({
      root: projectPath,
      output,
      noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],
      model: config.ai_summary_model,
      locale: config.locale,
      ghPagesBranch: config.gh_pages_branch,
      cname: config.cname,
      webhookUrl: config.webhook_url,
      reportBaseUrl: config.report_base_url,
    })
    logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated at ${output}.`)
  } catch (err: unknown) {
    logger.error(`[${projectName}] Report generation failed: ${String(err)}`)
    writeAlert(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'report_failed', `Report generation failed for ${projectName}: ${String(err)}`)
  }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Sensitive pattern detection
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

const SENSITIVE_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
  /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
  /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
  /(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i,
  /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}/,
  /Bearer\s+\S{20,}/i,
]

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Main entry point
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export async function main(): Promise<number> {
  const config = loadConfig()
  setupLogging(config.log_level as LogLevel)
  const logger = getLogger()

  if (!acquireLock()) {
    logger.info('Another heartbeat instance is running. Exiting.')
    writeAlert('global', 'lock_held', 'Heartbeat exited: another instance held the lock.')
    return 0
  }

  try {
    return await runHeartbeat(config)
  } finally {
    releaseLock()
  }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Heartbeat orchestration
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export async function runHeartbeat(config: Config): Promise<number> {
  const logger = getLogger()

  if (config.projects.length === 0) {
    logger.info('No projects registered in config.toml. Nothing to do.')
    return 0
  }

  logger.info(`Heartbeat started. ${config.projects.length} project(s) registered.`)

  for (const entry of config.projects) {
    const projectPath = resolve(entry.path)
    if (!existsSync(projectPath)) {
      logger.warn(`Project path does not exist, skipping: ${projectPath}`)
      continue
    }
    try {
      await processProject(config, projectPath, entry.name || (projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() ?? ''))
    } catch (err: unknown) {
      logger.exception(`Error processing project: ${projectPath}`, err)
      writeAlertWithThreshold(
        toPosixPath(projectPath),
        'push_failed',
        `Heartbeat error for project: ${projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() ?? ''}`,
      )
    }
  }

  logger.info('Heartbeat finished.')
  return 0
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Per-project processing
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export async function processProject(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string): Promise<void> {
  const logger = getLogger()
  const repoSlug = slugify(projectName, 'project')
  const globalRoot = defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()

  const discovered = discoverSourceFiles()
  let importedCount = 0

  for (const [client, source] of discovered) {
    let parsed: ParsedTranscript
    try {
      parsed = parseTranscript(client, source)
    } catch {
      logger.debug(`Failed to parse ${source}, skipping.`)
      continue
    }

    if (!transcriptHasContent(parsed)) continue
    if (!transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed, projectPath, repoSlug)) continue

    scanSensitive(parsed, projectPath)

    try {
      await importTranscript(parsed, projectPath, globalRoot, repoSlug, 'not-set', true)
      importedCount++
    } catch {
      logger.exception(`Failed to import transcript: ${source}`)
    }
  }

  if (importedCount === 0) {
    logger.info(`[${projectName}] No new transcripts to import.`)
  } else {
    logger.info(`[${projectName}] Imported ${importedCount} transcript(s).`)
    gitCommitAndPush(config, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)
  }

  if (config.generate_report) {
    await runBuildReport(config, projectPath, projectName)
  }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Sensitive pattern scanning
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function scanSensitive(parsed: ParsedTranscript, projectPath: string): void {
  const logger = getLogger()
  for (const msg of parsed.messages) {
    for (const pattern of SENSITIVE_PATTERNS) {
      if (pattern.test(msg.text)) {
        logger.warn(
          `Sensitive pattern detected in transcript ${parsed.source_path} (project: ${projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() ?? ''}, role: ${msg.role})`,
        )
        writeAlert(
          toPosixPath(projectPath),
          'sensitive_match',
          `Sensitive pattern in transcript: ${parsed.source_path.split(/[/\\]/).pop() ?? ''}`,
        )
        return
      }
    }
  }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Git operations
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function gitCommitAndPush(config: Config, projectPath: string, projectName: string, count: number): void {
  const logger = getLogger()

  const status = git(projectPath, 'status', '--porcelain', 'Memory/')
  if (!status.trim()) {
    logger.info(`[${projectName}] No git changes in Memory/.`)
    return
  }

  git(projectPath, 'add', 'Memory/')
  git(projectPath, 'commit', '-m', `memorytree(transcripts): import ${count} transcript(s)`)
  logger.info(`[${projectName}] Committed ${count} transcript import(s).`)

  if (!config.auto_push) {
    logger.info(`[${projectName}] auto_push disabled, skipping push.`)
    return
  }

  const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
  if (!remotes.trim()) {
    logger.warn(`[${projectName}] No git remote configured, skipping push.`)
    writeAlert(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'no_remote', 'Push skipped: no Git remote configured.')
    return
  }

  if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
    logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Push failed, retrying once...`)
    if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
      logger.error(`[${projectName}] Push failed after retry.`)
      writeAlertWithThreshold(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed', 'Push failed after retry.')
      return
    }
  }

  resetFailureCount(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed')
}

export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
  try {
    git(projectPath, 'push')
    getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
    return true
  } catch {
    return false
  }
}

Output:

  11: 1. `heartbeat.py` executes once and exits. It is stateless and idempotent 鈥?safe to re-run at any interval.
  12: 2. The OS-native scheduler invokes `heartbeat.py` on a fixed interval. Use cron (Linux), launchd (macOS), or Task Scheduler (Windows).
  13: 3. One computer runs exactly one scheduled heartbeat task. Multiple registrations on the same machine are not supported.

assistant #181 final_answer 05:40:39

1. 不能按当前设计让 http://localhost:10086/ 跟着 heartbeat 常驻启动。原因很直接：heartbeat 是“执行一轮就退出”的定时任务，不是常驻服务，skill 文档也明确写了这一点，见 [heartbeat-scheduling.md](C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/references/heartbeat-scheduling.md#L11)。在本项目里，heartbeat 最多只会在 generate_report=true 时重建静态报告，不会启动 report serve，见 [heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts#L161)。而 report serve 本身是一个持续监听端口的 HTTP 进程，见 [cmd-report.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts#L113)。所以把它塞进每 5 分钟跑一次的 heartbeat 里，不是合适模型。

更准确的做法有两个：
- 让 heartbeat 负责“自动更新报告文件”，也就是把 generate_report 打开。
- 让一个单独的常驻任务负责 openmnemo report serve，这样 localhost:10086 才会一直可访问。

2. memorytree-workflow 这套 heartbeat 也没有注册。我直接用 skill 自带的 CLI 查了状态，结果是 Registered: no。它在 Windows 下用的计划任务名也是 MemoryTree Heartbeat，见 [cmd-daemon.ts](C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L19) 和 [cmd-daemon.ts](C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L93)。我同时查了系统任务列表，也没有这个任务。当前全局配置只是写了参数，还没注册到系统：heartbeat_interval="5m"、auto_push=true、generate_report=false，见 [config.toml](C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/config.toml#L1)。

如果你要，我下一步可以直接做其中一种：
- 只注册 heartbeat。
- 注册 heartbeat，并把 generate_report 打开。
- 另外再单独加一个开机自启的本地 report serve，让 http://localhost:10086/ 常驻可访问。

你要我现在按哪种做？

token_count #182 05:40:39
total_tokens=703205
total 703205in 689032out 14173reason 8719

Show task summary

1. 不能按当前设计让 http://localhost:10086/ 跟着 heartbeat 常驻启动。原因很直接：heartbeat 是“执行一轮就退出”的定时任务，不是常驻服务，skill 文档也明确写了这一点，见 [heartbeat-scheduling.md](C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/references/heartbeat-scheduling.md#L11)。在本项目里，heartbeat 最多只会在 generate_report=true 时重建静态报告，不会启动 report serve，见 [heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts#L161)。而 report serve 本身是一个持续监听端口的 HTTP 进程，见 [cmd-report.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts#L113)。所以把它塞进每 5 分钟跑一次的 heartbeat 里，不是合适模型。

更准确的做法有两个：
- 让 heartbeat 负责“自动更新报告文件”，也就是把 generate_report 打开。
- 让一个单独的常驻任务负责 openmnemo report serve，这样 localhost:10086 才会一直可访问。

2. memorytree-workflow 这套 heartbeat 也没有注册。我直接用 skill 自带的 CLI 查了状态，结果是 Registered: no。它在 Windows 下用的计划任务名也是 MemoryTree Heartbeat，见 [cmd-daemon.ts](C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L19) 和 [cmd-daemon.ts](C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts#L93)。我同时查了系统任务列表，也没有这个任务。当前全局配置只是写了参数，还没注册到系统：heartbeat_interval="5m"、auto_push=true、generate_report=false，见 [config.toml](C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/config.toml#L1)。

如果你要，我下一步可以直接做其中一种：
- 只注册 heartbeat。
- 注册 heartbeat，并把 generate_report 打开。
- 另外再单独加一个开机自启的本地 report serve，让 http://localhost:10086/ 常驻可访问。

你要我现在按哪种做？

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"

  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #188 final_answer 05:42:03

通俗说：

现在有两个东西，不是一回事。

1. heartbeat
它像“定时清洁工”。
每隔几分钟起来干一次活，比如：
- 扫描有没有新的对话记录
- 导入到 Memory/
- 可选地重新生成报告页面

干完它就走，不会一直开着。

2. http://localhost:10086/
它像“门店前台”。
必须有一个程序一直在那儿值班，浏览器才能打开这个地址。

所以你现在打不开 http://localhost:10086/，不是因为 heartbeat 坏了，而是因为“前台没人值班”。也就是本地网页服务没启动。

再说你问的两点：

1. 能不能跟着 heartbeat 一起启动访问页面？
按现在这套设计，不能直接等同。
因为 heartbeat 是“定时跑一下就退出”，不是“长期挂着提供网页”。

最合理的理解是：
- heartbeat 负责“更新页面内容”
- report serve 负责“把页面展示出来”

2. memorytree-workflow 里的心跳注册了吗？
没有注册。
我已经查过了，这台机器上当前没有注册那个 Windows 定时任务。

一句话总结：

- 心跳：没注册
- 本地网页：也没启动
- 所以 localhost:10086 打不开是正常的

如果你愿意，下一步我可以帮你做成最省心的方案：
- 心跳负责自动更新报告
- 再单独配一个常驻本地网页服务
这样你以后打开 http://localhost:10086/ 就稳定了。

token_count #189 05:42:04
total_tokens=761469
total 761469in 746932out 14537reason 8719

通俗说：

现在有两个东西，不是一回事。

1. heartbeat
它像“定时清洁工”。
每隔几分钟起来干一次活，比如：
- 扫描有没有新的对话记录
- 导入到 Memory/
- 可选地重新生成报告页面

干完它就走，不会一直开着。

2. http://localhost:10086/
它像“门店前台”。
必须有一个程序一直在那儿值班，浏览器才能打开这个地址。

所以你现在打不开 http://localhost:10086/，不是因为 heartbeat 坏了，而是因为“前台没人值班”。也就是本地网页服务没启动。

再说你问的两点：

1. 能不能跟着 heartbeat 一起启动访问页面？
按现在这套设计，不能直接等同。
因为 heartbeat 是“定时跑一下就退出”，不是“长期挂着提供网页”。

最合理的理解是：
- heartbeat 负责“更新页面内容”
- report serve 负责“把页面展示出来”

2. memorytree-workflow 里的心跳注册了吗？
没有注册。
我已经查过了，这台机器上当前没有注册那个 Windows 定时任务。

一句话总结：

- 心跳：没注册
- 本地网页：也没启动
- 所以 localhost:10086 打不开是正常的

如果你愿意，下一步我可以帮你做成最省心的方案：
- 心跳负责自动更新报告
- 再单独配一个常驻本地网页服务
这样你以后打开 http://localhost:10086/ 就稳定了。

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

我已经看到远端最近几次升级点，重点不是“小修小补”，而是三块逻辑增强：report_port、基于 worktree 的 heartbeat 同步、以及默认 memorytree/* 分支和自动 upstream。现在我对照 openmnemo 看这些点是不是已经有、差在哪儿。

assistant #227 commentary 02:25:51

我已经对上了：远端 memorytree-workflow 的升级方向，核心是“更安全地自动写仓库”和“把本地访问端口配置化”。当前 openmnemo 还没有这些保护层，所以可以复用，但要分层迁移，不能整块照搬。

 248: // Write a VBScript launcher that runs node silently (no console window).
 249: // wscript.exe /B runs in batch mode (suppresses script error popups).
 250: // VBScript Run(..., 0, False): 0 = SW_HIDE hides the window; False = fire-and-forget.
 251: // Written as UTF-16 LE with BOM so wscript.exe handles non-ASCII paths (e.g. Chinese
 253: export function vbsLauncherPath(): string {
 254:   return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'heartbeat-launcher.vbs')
 257: export function writeVbsLauncher(scriptPath: string): string {
 258:   const vbsPath = vbsLauncherPath()
 259:   mkdirSync(dirname(vbsPath), { recursive: true })
 260:   // Inside VBScript strings, double-quote is escaped as ""

 159: | Git-safe behavior | Keeps automatic repo writes on dedicated `memorytree/*` branches and avoids polluting normal product branches |

assistant #295 commentary 02:28:17

我再补一个和你当前环境直接相关的差异：memorytree-workflow 在 Windows 上把 heartbeat 包成了静默启动的 VBS launcher，这个对你这种本机常驻场景是有实际价值的。openmnemo 现在还是直接让计划任务跑 node ... daemon run-once。

assistant #315 final_answer 02:30:01

看了，结论是：**可以复用，而且值得复用，但要“挑逻辑搬”，不要整套照抄。** **最值得复用的 3 块** - **Heartbeat 的仓库隔离写入逻辑。** `memorytree-workflow` 新版已经把 `development_path`、`memory_path`、`memory_branch` 拆开，并用 `git worktree` 保证自动写入落在专用 memory 分支，再把需要的内容同步回开发目录。这...

Expand full message

看了，结论是：可以复用，而且值得复用，但要“挑逻辑搬”，不要整套照抄。

最值得复用的 3 块
- Heartbeat 的仓库隔离写入逻辑。 memorytree-workflow 新版已经把 development_path、memory_path、memory_branch 拆开，并用 git worktree 保证自动写入落在专用 memory 分支，再把需要的内容同步回开发目录。这比你们现在 openmnemo 直接在当前项目目录里 git add Memory/、commit、push 安全很多。当前 openmnemo 的直接提交逻辑在 [heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts#L193)。GitHub 对应逻辑在：
- [config.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/config.ts)
- [worktree.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts)
- [sync.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/sync.ts)
- [heartbeat.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts)

- report_port 配置化。 新版 memorytree-workflow 已经把报告端口做成配置项，并且 report serve 会优先从配置里取端口。这一点很适合你们复用到 openmnemo。你们当前还是 CLI 默认写死 10086，见 [main.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/main.ts#L278) 和 [config.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/config.ts#L152)。GitHub 对应逻辑在：
- [config.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/config.ts)
- [main.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/cli/main.ts)

- Windows 下 daemon 静默启动。 memorytree-workflow 用 VBS launcher 包了一层 wscript.exe，避免计划任务每次弹黑窗。这个对你们当前 Windows 使用场景很实用。你们现在还是直接 /tr "node ... daemon run-once"，见 [cmd-daemon.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/cmd-daemon.ts#L247)。GitHub 仓库对应代码在 [cmd-daemon.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts)。

但有一块不要误解
- memorytree-workflow 的升级并没有把 heartbeat 变成常驻网页服务。它解决的是“定时同步、更安全写仓库、端口配置化”，不是“后台一直托管 localhost”。也就是说，即使复用它的新版逻辑，heartbeat 仍然主要是定时跑任务；如果你要稳定打开本地地址，还是需要单独的常驻 report serve 进程。GitHub README 和 CLI 入口都还是这个模型：
- [README.md](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/README.md)
- [main.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/cli/main.ts)

我建议你们在 openmnemo 的复用顺序
- 先搬 report_port 和 resolveReportPort，这是最小改动、立刻有用。
- 再搬 worktree + branch isolation + auto upstream，这是价值最大的 heartbeat 升级。
- 最后再搬 Windows 静默 daemon。
- 不建议直接搬整套 MemoryTree 的 AGENTS/归档政策文本，因为 openmnemo 已经是独立产品，不该被 skill 规则反向绑定。

一句话说，就是：可复用的不是“MemoryTree 这套文案”，而是它新版 heartbeat 的“隔离执行模型”。
如果你要，我下一步可以直接给你列一个 openmnemo 的落地迁移清单，精确到改哪些文件。

token_count #316 02:30:01
total_tokens=1435762
total 1435762in 1407696out 28066reason 18894
task_completed #317 019d090e-c473-71b3-a734-11d087adaa13 02:30:01

看了，结论是：可以复用，而且值得复用，但要“挑逻辑搬”，不要整套照抄。

最值得复用的 3 块
- Heartbeat 的仓库隔离写入逻辑。 memorytree-workflow 新版已经把 development_path、memory_path、memory_branch 拆开，并用 git worktree 保证自动写入落在专用 memory 分支，再把需要的内容同步回开发目录。这比你们现在 openmnemo 直接在当前项目目录里 git add Memory/、commit、push 安全很多。当前 openmnemo 的直接提交逻辑在 [heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts#L193)。GitHub 对应逻辑在：
- [config.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/config.ts)
- [worktree.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/worktree.ts)
- [sync.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/sync.ts)
- [heartbeat.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts)

- report_port 配置化。 新版 memorytree-workflow 已经把报告端口做成配置项，并且 report serve 会优先从配置里取端口。这一点很适合你们复用到 openmnemo。你们当前还是 CLI 默认写死 10086，见 [main.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/main.ts#L278) 和 [config.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/config.ts#L152)。GitHub 对应逻辑在：
- [config.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/heartbeat/config.ts)
- [main.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/cli/main.ts)

- Windows 下 daemon 静默启动。 memorytree-workflow 用 VBS launcher 包了一层 wscript.exe，避免计划任务每次弹黑窗。这个对你们当前 Windows 使用场景很实用。你们现在还是直接 /tr "node ... daemon run-once"，见 [cmd-daemon.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/cmd-daemon.ts#L247)。GitHub 仓库对应代码在 [cmd-daemon.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts)。

但有一块不要误解
- memorytree-workflow 的升级并没有把 heartbeat 变成常驻网页服务。它解决的是“定时同步、更安全写仓库、端口配置化”，不是“后台一直托管 localhost”。也就是说，即使复用它的新版逻辑，heartbeat 仍然主要是定时跑任务；如果你要稳定打开本地地址，还是需要单独的常驻 report serve 进程。GitHub README 和 CLI 入口都还是这个模型：
- [README.md](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/README.md)
- [main.ts](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/src/cli/main.ts)

我建议你们在 openmnemo 的复用顺序
- 先搬 report_port 和 resolveReportPort，这是最小改动、立刻有用。
- 再搬 worktree + branch isolation + auto upstream，这是价值最大的 heartbeat 升级。
- 最后再搬 Windows 静默 daemon。
- 不建议直接搬整套 MemoryTree 的 AGENTS/归档政策文本，因为 openmnemo 已经是独立产品，不该被 skill 规则反向绑定。

一句话说，就是：可复用的不是“MemoryTree 这套文案”，而是它新版 heartbeat 的“隔离执行模型”。
如果你要，我下一步可以直接给你列一个 openmnemo 的落地迁移清单，精确到改哪些文件。

{
  "turn_id": "019d0916-900e-7ea2-939b-738ba71af634",

  "timezone": "Asia/Shanghai",
  "approval_policy": "never",
  "sandbox_policy": {
    "type": "danger-full-access"
  },

      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #323 final_answer 02:33:41

可以。用最白话的说法：

结论先说
memorytree-workflow 这次升级，有些思路很值得我们拿来用，但不能整套直接搬过来。

你可以把它理解成：

- memorytree-workflow 更像一套“仓库记忆管理后台”
- openmnemo 更像一个“正式产品/工具”

所以我们适合借它的“后台做事方法”，不适合把它整套规则和壳子一起抄过来。

哪些能借
1. 后台定时任务怎么跑得更稳
就是 heartbeat / daemon 这套。这个能借。

2. Windows 上怎么安静运行
它对 Windows 做得更细，适合复用，避免计划任务老弹黑框。

3. 自动同步时怎么更安全
核心思想是：不要随便污染主开发分支。这个思路非常值得用。

哪些不能指望它直接解决
1. 它不会自动把 localhost 变成一直可访问
它主要是“定时干活”，不是“长期开网页服务”。

2. 它不是拿来直接替代 openmnemo 的
它更像内部工作流工具，不是你们面向用户的产品主体。

再打个比方
- openmnemo 是“店面”
- memorytree-workflow 是“店里的后勤系统”

后勤系统可以升级仓库、补货、整理账本。
但它不能直接替代店面营业。

所以我们的正确做法
不是“把 memorytree-workflow 整个搬过来”，而是：

- 借它的 heartbeat/daemon 逻辑
- 借它的 Windows 后台运行方式
- 借它的“别污染主分支”的安全思路
- 但 openmnemo 继续保留自己的 CLI、自己的产品结构

还有一个我想说清楚的点
你如果特别关心“本地访问端口可配置”这件事，memorytree-workflow 确实朝这个方向在升级；但我目前更建议把这块当成“可参考设计”，然后我们自己在 openmnemo 里完整落地，不要机械照搬。

一句话总结：

能复用，但复用的是“后台逻辑”和“安全策略”；不是把整个 memorytree-workflow 当成 openmnemo 本体。

如果你要，我下一步可以直接给你出一个非常通俗的迁移方案：

- 第一阶段先复用什么
- 第二阶段再复用什么
- 哪些先别动

我可以按“最省事版本”给你列。

token_count #324 02:33:41
total_tokens=1509748
total 1509748in 1479401out 30347reason 20566
task_completed #325 019d0916-900e-7ea2-939b-738ba71af634 02:33:41
可以。用最白话的说法： **结论先说** `memorytree-workflow` 这次升级，**有些思路很值得我们拿来用**，但**不能整套直接搬过来**。 你可以把它理解成： - `memorytree-workflow` 更像一套“仓库记忆管理后台” - `openmnemo` 更像一个“正式产品/工具” 所以我们适合借它的“后台做事方法”，不适合把它整套规则和壳子一起抄过来。 **哪些能借** 1. 后台定时任务怎么跑得更稳 就是 heartbeat / da...

结论先说
memorytree-workflow 这次升级，有些思路很值得我们拿来用，但不能整套直接搬过来。

你可以把它理解成：

- memorytree-workflow 更像一套“仓库记忆管理后台”
- openmnemo 更像一个“正式产品/工具”

所以我们适合借它的“后台做事方法”，不适合把它整套规则和壳子一起抄过来。

哪些能借
1. 后台定时任务怎么跑得更稳
就是 heartbeat / daemon 这套。这个能借。

2. Windows 上怎么安静运行
它对 Windows 做得更细，适合复用，避免计划任务老弹黑框。

3. 自动同步时怎么更安全
核心思想是：不要随便污染主开发分支。这个思路非常值得用。

哪些不能指望它直接解决

- 哪些先别动

我可以按“最省事版本”给你列。

Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #331 final_answer 02:38:35

可以，而且**我更推荐你说的这个方案**。 通俗讲： - `heartbeat` 负责“更新内容” - `Caddy` 负责“长期开门营业” 这两个角色分开，反而是最顺的。 **你问的“端口可以配置”是什么意思** 我说的不是“必须改代码才能换端口”，而是分两层： 1. 现在就已经能改 当前 `openmnemo` 启动本地服务时，本来就支持 `--port`，只是默认是 `10086`，见 [main.ts](D:/demo1/...

Expand full message

可以，而且我更推荐你说的这个方案。

通俗讲：

- heartbeat 负责“更新内容”
- Caddy 负责“长期开门营业”

这两个角色分开，反而是最顺的。

你问的“端口可以配置”是什么意思

我说的不是“必须改代码才能换端口”，而是分两层：

1. 现在就已经能改
当前 openmnemo 启动本地服务时，本来就支持 --port，只是默认是 10086，见 [main.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/main.ts#L280)。

2. 我前面说的“配置化”
意思是把端口写进统一配置，比如 report_port = 10086。
这样以后谁来启动本地页面，都默认读这一个配置，不用每次手工写 --port。

所以“可配置”本质上是：
- 现在：每次启动时临时指定
- 升级后：全局统一记住这个端口

能不能直接用 Caddy 常驻 10086
可以，完全可以。
而且对你们这个场景，比一直挂着 openmnemo report serve 更合适。

原因很简单：
- 你们生成的是静态 HTML 报告
- Caddy 很适合长期托管静态文件
- heartbeat 只要负责定时重新生成 Memory/07_reports
- Caddy 一直把这个目录发布在 10086 端口上

也就是说，未来最合理的结构是：

1. heartbeat 定时更新 [Memory/07_reports](D:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/07_reports)
2. Caddy 常驻监听 10086
3. 浏览器一直访问 http://localhost:10086/

这两者不冲突，反而是标准搭配。

最关键的一句话
heartbeat 不是网页服务器。
它不会替代 Caddy。
它更像“定时生成网页内容的人”。

Caddy 才是“把网页一直挂出来的人”。

所以你这个理解是对的
我们不用纠结“heartbeat 能不能自己长期开网页”。
直接让：
- heartbeat 负责“更新”
- Caddy 负责“常驻访问”

就行。

如果只讨论、不改代码
那我建议结论就是：

- memorytree-workflow 里值得复用的是：
- 更安全的 heartbeat 逻辑
- Windows 后台静默运行方式
- 端口统一配置思路
- 本地长期访问这件事：
- 直接交给 Caddy
- 不需要 heartbeat 来承担

如果以后真落地
最省事的方式不是改很多代码，而是：

:10086 {
  root * D:\demo1\openmnemo\Memory\07_reports
  file_server
}

这只是 Caddy 配置思路，不是改你们产品代码。

当前代码里也能看出这两个职责是分开的：
- report serve 只是本地 HTTP 服务：[cmd-report.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts#L113)
- heartbeat 只会在 generate_report=true 时生成报告，不会常驻开端口：[heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts#L161)

参考：
- memorytree-workflow 仓库：https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow
- 说明文档：https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/README.md

如果你愿意，我下一条可以只做“方案讨论”，直接给你画出一个你们适合的最终架构：
heartbeat + Caddy + 未来公网发布
不写代码，只讲怎么搭最合理。

total 1585326in 1551776out 33550reason 22891

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"

  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }

assistant #339 final_answer 02:44:39

**推荐架构** 最适合你们的形态是三层分工： - `openmnemo / heartbeat`：定时发现新会话、导入、生成报告 - `Caddy`：本地常驻把 `Memory/07_reports` 挂在 `10086` - 未来公网发布：把同一份静态报告输出再发布到 `GitHub Pages` 或别的静态托管 也就是说，浏览器一直访问 `http://localhost:10086/`，但背后不是 heartbeat 在撑...

Expand full message

推荐架构

最适合你们的形态是三层分工：

- openmnemo / heartbeat：定时发现新会话、导入、生成报告
- Caddy：本地常驻把 Memory/07_reports 挂在 10086
- 未来公网发布：把同一份静态报告输出再发布到 GitHub Pages 或别的静态托管

也就是说，浏览器一直访问 http://localhost:10086/，但背后不是 heartbeat 在撑着端口，而是 Caddy 在撑着。heartbeat 只负责把内容更新到磁盘。

为什么这样最合理

因为你们的报告本质上是静态文件。当前代码里 heartbeat 只会在 generate_report=true 时重建报告，不会常驻开网页服务，见 [heartbeat.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/heartbeat.ts#L161)。而本地网页服务是另一条命令，见 [cmd-report.ts](D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts#L113)。

所以最佳职责划分是：

- heartbeat 像“定时更新内容的人”
- Caddy 像“24 小时营业的前台”

这样有几个好处：

- 本地地址稳定，10086 可以一直开着
- 不需要让 heartbeat 伪装成常驻 Web 服务
- 以后做公网发布时，还是复用同一份 Memory/07_reports
- openmnemo report serve 可以保留成开发/调试工具，不必承担正式常驻职责

“端口可配置”到底要不要

如果你们用 Caddy 常驻 10086，那 openmnemo 里把端口做成配置项就不是刚需了，只是“锦上添花”。

通俗说：

- 用 Caddy 的情况下，真正对外监听 10086 的是 Caddy
- openmnemo 自己的 report serve 端口是多少，影响就没那么大了
- 所以 report_port 这个思路可以借，但优先级不高

换句话说，你们现在完全可以：

- 不改 openmnemo 端口逻辑
- 直接让 Caddy 常驻 10086
- heartbeat 只负责更新 Memory/07_reports

从 memorytree-workflow 借什么

建议只借这几块精华：

- 更安全的 heartbeat 写仓库方式：自动写入放到专用 memorytree/* 分支或 worktree，不污染主开发分支
参考仓库：
https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow
- Windows 下 daemon 静默运行，避免计划任务弹黑窗
参考 README：
https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/README.md
- 配置化思路，比如 report_port、report_base_url、gh_pages_branch

不建议借什么

- 不要让 heartbeat 自己去承担长期网页服务
- 不要把整个 skill 规则体系直接塞进 openmnemo
- 不要让产品主分支承受自动 Memory 提交

我给你的建议顺序

1. 架构上先定死：heartbeat 更新内容 + Caddy 常驻 10086
2. 然后再升级 heartbeat 的安全性：借 memorytree-workflow 的 branch/worktree 隔离逻辑
3. 最后再考虑公网发布：gh-pages 或静态站点托管

一句话结论

你们最好的终态，不是“让 heartbeat 负责网页访问”，而是：

heartbeat 产出内容 -> Caddy 常驻本地访问 -> 未来同一份内容再发公网

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续只做“方案讨论”，给你画一个更具体的落地版本：
“现在就能做的最小方案”和“后面代码升级版方案”。

total_tokens=1660500
total 1660500in 1625035out 35465reason 23907

**推荐架构** 最适合你们的形态是三层分工： - `openmnemo / heartbeat`：定时发现新会话、导入、生成报告 - `Caddy`：本地常驻把 `Memory/07_reports` 挂在 `10086` - 未来公网发布：把同一份静态报告输出再发布到 `GitHub Pages` 或别的静态托管 也就是说，浏览器一直访问 `http://localhost:10086/`，但背后不是 heartbeat 在撑着端口，而是 Caddy 在撑着。hea...

推荐架构

最适合你们的形态是三层分工：

- openmnemo / heartbeat：定时发现新会话、导入、生成报告
- Caddy：本地常驻把 Memory/07_reports 挂在 10086
- 未来公网发布：把同一份静态报告输出再发布到 GitHub Pages 或别的静态托管

也就是说，浏览器一直访问 http://localhost:10086/，但背后不是 heartbeat 在撑着端口，而是 Caddy 在撑着。heartbeat 只负责把内容更新到磁盘。

这样有几个好处：

通俗说：

- 用 Caddy 的情况下，真正对外监听 10086 的是 Caddy
- openmnemo 自己的 report serve 端口是多少，影响就没那么大了
- 所以 report_port 这个思路可以借，但优先级不高

换句话说，你们现在完全可以：

- 直接让 Caddy 常驻 10086
- heartbeat 只负责更新 Memory/07_reports

从 memorytree-workflow 借什么

建议只借这几块精华：

- 更安全的 heartbeat 写仓库方式：自动写入放到专用 memorytree/* 分支或 worktree，不污染主开发分支
参考仓库：
https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow
- Windows 下 daemon 静默运行，避免计划任务弹黑窗
参考 README：
https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/blob/main/README.md
- 配置化思路，比如 report_port、report_base_url、gh_pages_branch

不建议借什么

- 不要让 heartbeat 自己去承担长期网页服务
- 不要把整个 skill 规则体系直接塞进 openmnemo
- 不要让产品主分支承受自动 Memory 提交

我给你的建议顺序

1. 架构上先定死：heartbeat 更新内容 + Caddy 常驻 10086
2. 然后再升级 heartbeat 的安全性：借 memorytree-workflow 的 branch/worktree 隔离逻辑
3. 最后再考虑公网发布：gh-pages 或静态站点托管

一句话结论

你们最好的终态，不是“让 heartbeat 负责网页访问”，而是：

heartbeat 产出内容 -> Caddy 常驻本地访问 -> 未来同一份内容再发公网

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续只做“方案讨论”，给你画一个更具体的落地版本：
“现在就能做的最小方案”和“后面代码升级版方案”。

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Output:
/**
 * Load, validate, and manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml.
 * Port of scripts/_config_utils.py
 */

import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
import { homedir } from 'node:os'
import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'

import { parse as parseToml } from 'smol-toml'

import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Constants
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

const DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = '5m'
const DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH = true
const DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL = 'info'
const DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT = false
const DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'
const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'en'
const DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH = ''
const DEFAULT_CNAME = ''
const DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL = ''
const DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL = ''
const DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT = 10010
const VALID_LOG_LEVELS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error'])

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Types
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export interface ProjectEntry {
  readonly path: string
  readonly name: string
}

export interface Config {
  readonly heartbeat_interval: string
  readonly watch_dirs: readonly string[]
  readonly projects: readonly ProjectEntry[]
  readonly auto_push: boolean
  readonly log_level: string
  readonly generate_report: boolean
  readonly ai_summary_model: string
  readonly locale: string
  readonly gh_pages_branch: string
  readonly cname: string
  readonly webhook_url: string
  /** Base URL for RSS/OG meta (e.g. 'https://memory.example.com'). Empty = skip. */
  readonly report_base_url: string
  /** Port for the report HTTP server. Default: 10010. */
  readonly report_port: number
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Path helpers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function memorytreeRoot(): string {
  return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree')
}

export function configPath(): string {
  return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'config.toml')
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Load / Save
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function loadConfig(): Config {
  const path = configPath()
  if (!existsSync(path)) {
    return defaultConfig()
  }
  let raw: Record<string, unknown>
  try {
    const text = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    raw = parseToml(text) as Record<string, unknown>
  } catch {
    return defaultConfig()
  }
  return parseRaw(raw)
}

export function saveConfig(cfg: Config): void {
  const path = configPath()
  mkdirSync(dirname(path), { recursive: true })

  const lines: string[] = [
    `heartbeat_interval = ${tomlString(cfg.heartbeat_interval)}`,
    `auto_push = ${cfg.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
    `log_level = ${tomlString(cfg.log_level)}`,
    `generate_report = ${cfg.generate_report ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
    `ai_summary_model = ${tomlString(cfg.ai_summary_model)}`,
    `locale = ${tomlString(cfg.locale ?? DEFAULT_LOCALE)}`,
    `gh_pages_branch = ${tomlString(cfg.gh_pages_branch ?? DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH)}`,
    `cname = ${tomlString(cfg.cname ?? DEFAULT_CNAME)}`,
    `webhook_url = ${tomlString(cfg.webhook_url ?? DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL)}`,
    `report_base_url = ${tomlString(cfg.report_base_url ?? DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL)}`,
    `report_port = ${cfg.report_port ?? DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT}`,
  ]

  if (cfg.watch_dirs.length > 0) {
    const items = cfg.watch_dirs.map(d => tomlString(d)).join(', ')
    lines.push(`watch_dirs = [${items}]`)
  } else {
    lines.push('watch_dirs = []')
  }

  lines.push('')
  for (const project of cfg.projects) {
    lines.push('[[projects]]')
    lines.push(`path = ${tomlString(project.path)}`)
    if (project.name) {
      lines.push(`name = ${tomlString(project.name)}`)
    }
Tool Result #369 shell_command call_AEdxh7lUr3XBKx2DMqG0BzKk exit 0 02:48:52
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: lines.push('') } writeFileSync(path, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8') } // ---------------------------------------------------------...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
    lines.push('')
  }

  writeFileSync(path, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Interval parsing
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function intervalToSeconds(interval: string): number {
  const match = interval.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
  if (!match) {
    return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
  }
  const value = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
  const unit = match[2]!
  if (value <= 0) {
    return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
  }
  const multiplier: Record<string, number> = { s: 1, m: 60, h: 3600 }
  return value * (multiplier[unit] ?? 60)
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Project registration
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

export function registerProject(cfg: Config, repoPath: string): Config {
  const resolved = toPosixPath(resolve(repoPath))
  for (const entry of cfg.projects) {
    if (toPosixPath(resolve(entry.path)) === resolved) {
      return cfg
    }
  }
  const name = resolved.split('/').pop() ?? ''
  return {
    ...cfg,
    projects: [...cfg.projects, { path: resolved, name }],
  }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Internal helpers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function defaultConfig(): Config {
  return {
    heartbeat_interval: DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
    watch_dirs: [],
    projects: [],
    auto_push: DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH,
    log_level: DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL,
    generate_report: DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT,
    ai_summary_model: DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL,
    locale: DEFAULT_LOCALE,
    gh_pages_branch: DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH,
    cname: DEFAULT_CNAME,
    webhook_url: DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL,
    report_base_url: DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL,
    report_port: DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT,
  }
}

function parseRaw(raw: Record<string, unknown>): Config {
  let interval = raw['heartbeat_interval']
  if (typeof interval !== 'string' || !isValidInterval(interval)) {
    interval = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
  }

  let autoPush = raw['auto_push']
  if (typeof autoPush !== 'boolean') {
    autoPush = DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH
  }

  let logLevel = raw['log_level']
  if (typeof logLevel !== 'string' || !VALID_LOG_LEVELS.has(logLevel.toLowerCase())) {
    logLevel = DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL
  }
  logLevel = (logLevel as string).toLowerCase()

  const watchDirs: string[] = []
  const rawDirs = raw['watch_dirs']
  if (Array.isArray(rawDirs)) {
    for (const d of rawDirs) {
      if (typeof d === 'string') watchDirs.push(d)
    }
  }

  const projects: ProjectEntry[] = []
  const rawProjects = raw['projects']
  if (Array.isArray(rawProjects)) {
    for (const entry of rawProjects) {
      if (entry !== null && typeof entry === 'object' && !Array.isArray(entry)) {
        const rec = entry as Record<string, unknown>
        if (typeof rec['path'] === 'string') {
          projects.push({
            path: rec['path'] as string,
            name: String(rec['name'] ?? ''),
          })
        }
      }
    }
  }

  let generateReport = raw['generate_report']
  if (typeof generateReport !== 'boolean') {
    generateReport = DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT
  }

  let aiSummaryModel = raw['ai_summary_model']
  if (typeof aiSummaryModel !== 'string' || !aiSummaryModel) {
    aiSummaryModel = DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL
  }

  let locale = raw['locale']
  if (typeof locale !== 'string' || !locale) {
    locale = DEFAULT_LOCALE
  }

  let ghPagesBranch = raw['gh_pages_branch']
  if (typeof ghPagesBranch !== 'string') {
    ghPagesBranch = DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH
  }

  let cname = raw['cname']
  if (typeof cname !== 'string') {
    cname = DEFAULT_CNAME
  }

  let webhookUrl = raw['webhook_url']
  if (typeof webhookUrl !== 'string') {
    webhookUrl = DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL
  }

  let reportBaseUrl = raw['report_base_url']
  if (typeof reportBaseUrl !== 'string') {
    reportBaseUrl = DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL
  }

  return {
    heartbeat_interval: interval as string,
    watch_dirs: watchDirs,
    projects,
    auto_push: autoPush as boolean,
    log_level: logLevel as string,
    generate_report: generateReport as boolean,
    ai_summary_model: aiSummaryModel as string,
    locale: locale as string,
    gh_pages_branch: ghPagesBranch as string,
    cname: cname as string,
    webhook_url: webhookUrl as string,
    report_base_url: reportBaseUrl as string,
    report_port: typeof raw['report_port'] === 'number' && raw['report_port'] > 0
      ? raw['report_port']
      : DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT,
  }
}

function isValidInterval(value: string): boolean {
  const match = value.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
  if (!match) return false
  return parseInt(match[1]!, 10) > 0
}

function tomlString(value: string): string {
  const escaped = value.replace(/\\/g, '\\\\').replace(/"/g, '\\"')
  return `"${escaped}"`
}

Exit code: 0
